---
title: "South Australia Spring Frosts (2024)"
---

## Overview

Widespread frost events occurred across major grain-producing regions of South Australia during September and early October 2024. The most severe impacts were reported on the Eyre Peninsula and in the Mid North.

## Meteorological context

The frost events developed during a very dry growing season, with many regions experiencing well below average rainfall. Multiple frost events occurred over a short period, including at least five days below 0°C in mid-September, with minimum temperatures reported as low as −6.5°C.

The extended duration and frequency of these events contributed to significant agricultural impact.

## Spatial definition

This event is defined over major cropping regions of South Australia, particularly the Eyre Peninsula and Mid North, where the most severe impacts were reported.

A bounding box is used to represent the affected region for analysis with gridded datasets such as ERA5.

## Impact

The frost events affected a wide range of crops, including wheat, lentils, barley, and canola. Many crops were at flowering or early grain development stages when frost occurred, increasing sensitivity.

Reports indicate substantial yield losses, with some regions experiencing severe damage requiring crops to be cut for hay or abandoned.

## Notes and uncertainty

The spatial boundary is approximate and intended for regional-scale analysis.  
Localised frost severity likely varied depending on topography and soil type.

This event represents a compound stress scenario, with frost occurring alongside drought conditions.
